searchDocs.title: Visualize‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> DebugBuilder:Visualize(value:¬†boolean) ‚Üí¬†DebugBuilder Enables the trajectory visualizer. Draws cast segments, hit points, surface normals, bounce vectors, and corner trap markers in the world. Zero runtime cost when false. Default: false

searchDocs.title: Done‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> DebugBuilder:Done() ‚Üí¬†BehaviorBuilder Returns the root BehaviorBuilder.

searchDocs.title: Filter‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> BounceBuilder:Filter(callback:¬†( context:¬†BulletContext, result:¬†RaycastResult, velocity:¬†Vector3 )¬†‚Üí¬†boolean) ‚Üí¬†BounceBuilder Callback invoked for each raycast hit. Return true to allow the bullet to bounce off the surface. Must be synchronous ‚ÄĒ yielding inside this callback will cause the cast to be terminated with an error on the next frame. Signature: (context: BulletContext, result: RaycastResult, velocity: Vector3) -> boolean Default: nil (no bouncing)

searchDocs.title: ResetPierceOnBounce‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> BounceBuilder:ResetPierceOnBounce(value:¬†boolean) ‚Üí¬†BounceBuilder When true, pierce state (filter, PiercedInstances, PierceCount) is automatically reset after each confirmed bounce, restoring the full pierce budget for the new arc. Required for bounce + pierce combinations where the post-bounce trajectory should be able to re-detect previously pierced surfaces. For conditional resets (e.g. only after the first bounce), call cast:ResetPierceState() manually inside an OnBounce handler instead. Default: false

searchDocs.title: Max‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> BounceBuilder:Max(value:¬†number) ‚Üí¬†BounceBuilder Maximum total bounces across the bullet's entire lifetime. Default: 5

searchDocs.title: SpeedThreshold‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> BounceBuilder:SpeedThreshold(value:¬†number) ‚Üí¬†BounceBuilder Minimum speed in studs per second required for a bounce attempt. Below this speed the hit is treated as a terminal impact. Default: 20

searchDocs.title: Restitution‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> BounceBuilder:Restitution(value:¬†number) ‚Üí¬†BounceBuilder Energy-retention coefficient applied to reflected velocity. Must be in [0, 1]. 1.0 = perfectly elastic (no energy loss). 0.0 = bullet stops on first contact. Default: 0.7

searchDocs.title: MaterialRestitution‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> BounceBuilder:MaterialRestitution(value:¬†{[Enum.Material]:¬†number}) ‚Üí¬†BounceBuilder Per-material restitution multipliers, keyed by Enum.Material. Combined multiplicatively with the base restitution in BounceBuilder:Restitution. Omitted materials use a multiplier of 1.0. :MaterialRestitution({ [Enum.Material.Concrete] = 0.5, [Enum.Material.Plastic] = 0.95, }) Default: {}

searchDocs.title: NormalPerturbation‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> BounceBuilder:NormalPerturbation(value:¬†number) ‚Üí¬†BounceBuilder Adds random noise to the surface normal before reflecting, simulating rough or irregular surfaces. 0 = clean mirror reflection. Default: 0.0

searchDocs.title: Done‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> BounceBuilder:Done() ‚Üí¬†BehaviorBuilder Returns the root BehaviorBuilder.

searchDocs.title: Template‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> CosmeticBuilder:Template(value:¬†BasePart ) ‚Üí¬†CosmeticBuilder A BasePart that is cloned once per Vetra:Fire call and used as the visible bullet. The clone is destroyed automatically on termination. Mutually exclusive with :Provider() ‚ÄĒ Provider takes priority. Default: nil

searchDocs.title: Container‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> CosmeticBuilder:Container(value:¬†Instance ) ‚Üí¬†CosmeticBuilder Parent Instance for the cosmetic bullet object. Defaults to workspace if nil. Default: nil

searchDocs.title: Provider‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> CosmeticBuilder:Provider(callback:¬†()¬†‚Üí¬†Instance? ) ‚Üí¬†CosmeticBuilder A function called once per Vetra:Fire that returns the cosmetic bullet Instance. Use this for object pooling or procedural creation. Must be synchronous ‚ÄĒ yielding will log a warning. Takes priority over :Template() if both are set. Signature: () -> Instance? Default: nil

searchDocs.title: Done‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> CosmeticBuilder:Done() ‚Üí¬†BehaviorBuilder Returns the root BehaviorBuilder.

searchDocs.title: Filter‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> PierceBuilder:Filter(callback:¬†( context:¬†BulletContext, result:¬†RaycastResult, velocity:¬†Vector3 )¬†‚Üí¬†boolean) ‚Üí¬†PierceBuilder Callback invoked for each raycast hit. Return true to allow the bullet to pierce through the instance. Must be synchronous ‚ÄĒ yielding inside this callback will cause the cast to be terminated with an error on the next frame. Signature: (context: BulletContext, result: RaycastResult, velocity: Vector3) -> boolean Default: nil (no piercing)

searchDocs.title: Max‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> PierceBuilder:Max(value:¬†number) ‚Üí¬†PierceBuilder Maximum total number of surfaces the bullet can pierce over its lifetime. Default: 3

searchDocs.title: SpeedThreshold‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> PierceBuilder:SpeedThreshold(value:¬†number) ‚Üí¬†PierceBuilder Minimum speed in studs per second required for a pierce attempt. Below this speed the hit is treated as a solid terminal impact. Default: 50

searchDocs.title: SpeedRetention‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> PierceBuilder:SpeedRetention(value:¬†number) ‚Üí¬†PierceBuilder Fraction of speed retained after each pierce. Must be in [0, 1]. 0.8 means 20% of speed is lost per pierce ‚ÄĒ deeper chains become progressively slower. Default: 0.8

searchDocs.title: NormalBias‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> PierceBuilder:NormalBias(value:¬†number) ‚Üí¬†PierceBuilder Restricts piercing to impacts above a minimum head-on angle. Must be in [0, 1]. 1.0 = all angles allowed. 0.0 = only perfectly perpendicular impacts pierce. Default: 1.0

searchDocs.title: Done‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> PierceBuilder:Done() ‚Üí¬†BehaviorBuilder Returns the root BehaviorBuilder.

searchDocs.title: TimeThreshold‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> CornerTrapBuilder:TimeThreshold(value:¬†number) ‚Üí¬†CornerTrapBuilder Minimum time in seconds that must elapse between successive bounces. Two bounces closer together than this are flagged as a corner trap. Default: 0.002

searchDocs.title: NormalDotThreshold‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> CornerTrapBuilder:NormalDotThreshold(value:¬†number) ‚Üí¬†CornerTrapBuilder Dot-product threshold for the normal-opposition guard. Must be in [-1, 0]. If consecutive surface normals have a dot product below this value they are considered opposing (corner trap). -0.85 ‚Čą surfaces within ~32¬į of face-to-face. Default: -0.85

searchDocs.title: DisplacementThreshold‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> CornerTrapBuilder:DisplacementThreshold(value:¬†number) ‚Üí¬†CornerTrapBuilder Minimum stud distance between successive bounce contact points. Displacement below this threshold triggers the spatial-proximity guard. Default: 0.5

searchDocs.title: Done‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> CornerTrapBuilder:Done() ‚Üí¬†BehaviorBuilder Returns the root BehaviorBuilder.

searchDocs.title: new‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> BehaviorBuilder.new() ‚Üí¬†BehaviorBuilder Creates a new builder pre-populated with all default values.

searchDocs.title: Sniper‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> BehaviorBuilder.Sniper() ‚Üí¬†BehaviorBuilder Returns a pre-configured builder for a high-velocity, long-range, pierce-capable projectile. No bouncing. Suitable for rifles and anti-materiel weapons. Further chain methods before calling :Build() to customise.

searchDocs.title: Grenade‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> BehaviorBuilder.Grenade() ‚Üí¬†BehaviorBuilder Returns a pre-configured builder for a low-speed, gravity-affected, bouncy projectile. No piercing. Suitable for thrown grenades or bouncing explosives.

searchDocs.title: Pistol‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> BehaviorBuilder.Pistol() ‚Üí¬†BehaviorBuilder Returns a pre-configured builder for a standard short-to-mid range projectile with single pierce and no bounce. Suitable for handguns and SMGs.

searchDocs.title: Physics‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> BehaviorBuilder:Physics() ‚Üí¬†PhysicsBuilder Opens the Physics configuration group. Available setters: :MaxDistance(), :MinSpeed(), :Gravity(), :Acceleration(), :RaycastParams(). Call :Done() to return to the root builder.

searchDocs.title: Pierce‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> BehaviorBuilder:Pierce() ‚Üí¬†PierceBuilder Opens the Pierce configuration group. Available setters: :Filter(), :Max(), :SpeedThreshold(), :SpeedRetention(), :NormalBias(). Call :Done() to return.

searchDocs.title: Bounce‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> BehaviorBuilder:Bounce() ‚Üí¬†BounceBuilder Opens the Bounce configuration group. Available setters: :Filter(), :Max(), :SpeedThreshold(), :Restitution(), :MaterialRestitution(), :NormalPerturbation(). Call :Done() to return.

searchDocs.title: HighFidelity‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> BehaviorBuilder:HighFidelity() ‚Üí¬†HighFidelityBuilder Opens the HighFidelity configuration group. Available setters: :SegmentSize(), :FrameBudget(), :AdaptiveScale(), :MinSegmentSize(), :MaxBouncesPerFrame(). Call :Done() to return.

searchDocs.title: CornerTrap‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> BehaviorBuilder:CornerTrap() ‚Üí¬†CornerTrapBuilder Opens the CornerTrap configuration group. Available setters: :TimeThreshold(), :NormalDotThreshold(), :DisplacementThreshold(). Call :Done() to return.

searchDocs.title: Cosmetic‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> BehaviorBuilder:Cosmetic() ‚Üí¬†CosmeticBuilder Opens the Cosmetic configuration group. Available setters: :Template(), :Container(), :Provider(). Call :Done() to return.

searchDocs.title: Debug‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> BehaviorBuilder:Debug() ‚Üí¬†DebugBuilder Opens the Debug configuration group. Available setters: :Visualize(). Call :Done() to return.

searchDocs.title: Build‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> BehaviorBuilder:Build() ‚Üí¬†VetraBehavior?-- Frozen behavior table, or nil if validation failed. Validates the current configuration and returns a frozen VetraBehavior table ready to pass to Vetra:Fire. All validation errors are collected and logged together so every problem is reported at once. Returns nil if any validation error is found. Does not consume the builder ‚ÄĒ call :Build() multiple times to produce independent frozen tables from the same configuration.

searchDocs.title: MaxDistance‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> PhysicsBuilder:MaxDistance(value:¬†number) ‚Üí¬†PhysicsBuilder Maximum distance in studs the bullet can travel before expiring.OnHit fires with a nil RaycastResult when this is reached. Default: 500

searchDocs.title: MinSpeed‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> PhysicsBuilder:MinSpeed(value:¬†number) ‚Üí¬†PhysicsBuilder Minimum speed in studs per second. When the bullet's speed drops below this value it is terminated naturally.OnHit fires with a nil RaycastResult. Default: 1

searchDocs.title: Gravity‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> PhysicsBuilder:Gravity(value:¬†Vector3 ) ‚Üí¬†PhysicsBuilder Gravitational acceleration applied to the bullet. Pass a negative-Y vector for downward gravity. Default: Vector3.new(0, -workspace.Gravity, 0)

searchDocs.title: Acceleration‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> PhysicsBuilder:Acceleration(value:¬†Vector3 ) ‚Üí¬†PhysicsBuilder Extra constant acceleration layered on top of gravity (e.g. rocket thrust, wind). Default: Vector3.zero

searchDocs.title: RaycastParams‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> PhysicsBuilder:RaycastParams(value:¬†RaycastParams) ‚Üí¬†PhysicsBuilder The RaycastParams used for all raycasts during this cast's lifetime. The solver clones these internally ‚ÄĒ the original is never mutated. Default: RaycastParams.new()

searchDocs.title: Done‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> PhysicsBuilder:Done() ‚Üí¬†BehaviorBuilder Returns the root BehaviorBuilder.

searchDocs.title: CastTrajectory‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> interface CastTrajectory { StartTime:¬†number-- Runtime.TotalRuntime when this segment began. EndTime:¬†number-- Runtime.TotalRuntime when closed; -1 while active. Origin:¬†Vector3 -- World-space start position of this arc. InitialVelocity:¬†Vector3 -- Velocity at StartTime in studs/second. Acceleration:¬†Vector3 -- Constant acceleration for this arc (gravity + extra). } Represents one continuous parabolic arc of a projectile's flight path. A new segment is appended to Runtime.Trajectories on every bounce or mid-flight kinematic change via the Set* / Add* methods on VetraCast. EndTime of -1 indicates the segment is still active.

searchDocs.title: BulletSnapshot‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> interface BulletSnapshot { Id:¬†number-- Unique identifier. Origin:¬†Vector3 -- Muzzle position at fire time. Direction:¬†Vector3 -- Initial unit direction. Speed:¬†number-- Initial speed in studs/second. Position:¬†Vector3? -- Current world-space position, or nil before the first frame. Velocity:¬†Vector3 -- Current velocity vector. Alive:¬†boolean-- Whether the cast is still being simulated. Lifetime:¬†number-- Seconds elapsed since creation. DistanceTraveled:¬†number-- Straight-line distance from Origin to Position. } Read-only snapshot of a BulletContext's state at a point in time. Returned by BulletContext:GetSnapshot.

searchDocs.title: BulletContextConfig‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> interface BulletContextConfig { Origin:¬†Vector3 -- Required. World-space muzzle position. Direction:¬†Vector3 -- Required. Unit direction vector. Speed:¬†number-- Required. Initial speed in studs/second. Callbacks:¬†BulletCallbacks?-- Optional per-instance event callbacks. SolverData:¬†any?-- Internal ‚ÄĒ used by the solver to attach lifecycle hooks. } Configuration table passed to BulletContext.new.

searchDocs.title: VetraBehavior‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> interface VetraBehavior { Acceleration:¬†Vector3? -- Extra acceleration on top of gravity. Default: Vector3.zero MaxDistance:¬†number?-- Max flight distance in studs. Default: 500 RaycastParams:¬†RaycastParams?-- Raycast filter. Default: RaycastParams.new() Gravity:¬†Vector3? -- Gravitational acceleration. Default: workspace gravity downward. MinSpeed:¬†number?-- Termination speed threshold in studs/sec. Default: 1 CanPierceFunction:¬†(( BulletContext, RaycastResult, Vector3 )¬†‚Üí¬†boolean)?-- Pierce gate. Default: nil MaxPierceCount:¬†number?-- Lifetime pierce limit. Default: 3 PierceSpeedThreshold:¬†number?-- Min speed to attempt pierce. Default: 50 PenetrationSpeedRetention:¬†number?-- Speed fraction kept per pierce [0,1]. Default: 0.8 PierceNormalBias:¬†number?-- Min approach angle for pierce [0,1]. Default: 1.0 CanBounceFunction:¬†(( BulletContext, RaycastResult, Vector3 )¬†‚Üí¬†boolean)?-- Bounce gate. Default: nil MaxBounces:¬†number?-- Lifetime bounce limit. Default: 5 BounceSpeedThreshold:¬†number?-- Min speed to attempt bounce. Default: 20 Restitution:¬†number?-- Energy retention per bounce [0,1]. Default: 0.7 MaterialRestitution:¬†{[Enum.Material]:¬†number}?-- Per-material multipliers. Default: {} NormalPerturbation:¬†number?-- Random normal noise for rough surfaces. Default: 0.0 HighFidelitySegmentSize:¬†number?-- Sub-segment length in studs. Default: 0.5 HighFidelityFrameBudget:¬†number?-- Ms budget per cast per frame. Default: 4 AdaptiveScaleFactor:¬†number?-- Adaptive scaling multiplier > 1. Default: 1.5 MinSegmentSize:¬†number?-- Hard floor for adaptive sizing. Default: 0.1 MaxBouncesPerFrame:¬†number?-- Per-frame bounce cap. Default: 10 CornerTimeThreshold:¬†number?-- Min seconds between bounces. Default: 0.002 CornerNormalDotThreshold:¬†number?-- Normal opposition threshold [-1,0]. Default: -0.85 CornerDisplacementThreshold:¬†number?-- Min bounce separation in studs. Default: 0.5 CosmeticBulletTemplate:¬†BasePart? -- Part cloned per fire. Default: nil CosmeticBulletContainer:¬†Instance? -- Parent for cosmetic object. Default: nil CosmeticBulletProvider:¬†(()¬†‚Üí¬†Instance? )?-- Provider function (takes priority). Default: nil VisualizeCasts:¬†boolean?-- Enable trajectory visualizer. Default: false } Complete configuration for a projectile cast. All fields are optional ‚ÄĒ any omitted field falls back to a safe built-in default. Prefer constructing this via BehaviorBuilder rather than by hand to get typed setters, validation, and a frozen result. Physics Pierce Bounce High Fidelity Corner Trap Cosmetic Debug

searchDocs.title: Installation‚Äč
searchDocs.content: Drop the Vetra folder into ReplicatedStorage and require it from your weapon scripts.

searchDocs.title: Basic Setup‚Äč
searchDocs.content: local Vetra = require(ReplicatedStorage.Vetra) local BulletContext = require(ReplicatedStorage.Vetra.BulletContext) -- Create the solver once (connects the frame loop) local Solver = Vetra.new() local Signals = Solver:GetSignals() -- Connect to signals once at initialisation Signals.OnHit:Connect(function(context, result, velocity) if result then print("Hit", result.Instance.Name, "at", result.Position) else print("Bullet expired (distance or speed)") end end) Signals.OnBounce:Connect(function(context, result, velocity, bounceCount) print("Bounce #" .. bounceCount) end)

searchDocs.title: Firing a Bullet‚Äč
searchDocs.content: -- Build a behavior (do this once per weapon type, not per shot) local Behavior = Vetra.BehaviorBuilder.new() :Physics() :MaxDistance(500) :MinSpeed(5) :Done() :Bounce() :Max(3) :Restitution(0.7) :Filter(function(ctx, result, vel) return true -- bounce off everything end) :Done() :Build() -- Fire local context = BulletContext.new({ Origin = muzzlePosition, Direction = direction, Speed = 200, }) Solver:Fire(context, Behavior)

searchDocs.title: Using Presets‚Äč
searchDocs.content: BehaviorBuilder ships with three preset constructors as a starting point:  -- Sniper: long range, pierce-capable, high fidelity local SniperBehavior = Vetra.BehaviorBuilder.Sniper():Build() -- Grenade: low speed, bouncy, gravity-affected local GrenadeBehavior = Vetra.BehaviorBuilder.Grenade():Build() -- Pistol: standard range, single pierce local PistolBehavior = Vetra.BehaviorBuilder.Pistol():Build()   You can chain additional overrides on any preset before calling :Build():  local Behavior = Vetra.BehaviorBuilder.Sniper() :Physics() :MaxDistance(2000) :Done() :Build()

searchDocs.title: Attaching Metadata‚Äč
searchDocs.content: Use UserData to attach weapon-specific data that travels with the bullet and is available in every signal handler:  context.UserData.Damage = 75 context.UserData.ShooterId = Players.LocalPlayer.UserId Signals.OnHit:Connect(function(context, result, velocity) print("Damage:", context.UserData.Damage) print("Fired by:", context.UserData.ShooterId) end)

searchDocs.title: Terminating Early‚Äč
searchDocs.content: -- From a signal handler or any code holding the context context:Terminate()

searchDocs.title: Enabling the Visualizer‚Äč
searchDocs.content: Set VisualizeCasts = true in your behavior (or use :Debug():Visualize(true):Done()) to draw cast segments, normals, bounce vectors, and corner trap markers in the world. Has zero runtime cost when disabled.  local Behavior = Vetra.BehaviorBuilder.new() :Debug() :Visualize(true) :Done() :Build()

searchDocs.title: Alive‚Äč
searchDocs.content: This item is read only and cannot be modified. Read Only </> VetraCast.Alive: boolean true while the cast is being simulated. Set to false as the very first action in termination so re-entrant calls are no-ops.

searchDocs.title: Paused‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> VetraCast.Paused: boolean When true, _StepProjectile skips this cast entirely. TotalRuntime does not advance, producing seamless resumption from the paused position when set back to false.

searchDocs.title: UserData‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> VetraCast.UserData: {[any]:¬†any} Free-form table for weapon-specific metadata. Shared with the linkedBulletContext and passed unchanged on every signal emission.

searchDocs.title: GetPosition‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> VetraCast:GetPosition() ‚Üí¬†Vector3  Returns the bullet's current world-space position using the analytic kinematic formula. Always accurate to the current simulation time regardless of when in the frame it is called.

searchDocs.title: GetVelocity‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> VetraCast:GetVelocity() ‚Üí¬†Vector3  Returns the bullet's current velocity vector. The magnitude of this vector is the current speed in studs per second.

searchDocs.title: GetAcceleration‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> VetraCast:GetAcceleration() ‚Üí¬†Vector3  Returns the constant acceleration vector for the active trajectory segment. This is the pre-computed sum of gravity and any extra acceleration ‚ÄĒ notworkspace.Gravity alone.

searchDocs.title: SetPosition‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> VetraCast:SetPosition(position:¬†Vector3 ) ‚Üí¬†() Teleports the bullet to a new world-space position without changing its velocity or acceleration. Opens a new trajectory segment if simulation time has already elapsed on the current one.

searchDocs.title: SetVelocity‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> VetraCast:SetVelocity(velocity:¬†Vector3 ) ‚Üí¬†() Changes the bullet's velocity to the given vector. Opens a new trajectory segment if needed. TIP Call VetraCast:ResetBounceState after a sharp velocity change to prevent the corner-trap detector from triggering on the new trajectory.

searchDocs.title: SetAcceleration‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> VetraCast:SetAcceleration(acceleration:¬†Vector3 ) ‚Üí¬†() Replaces the bullet's constant acceleration for future simulation. Because acceleration is constant within a segment, this always opens a new segment unless the current one has zero elapsed time.

searchDocs.title: AddPosition‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> VetraCast:AddPosition(offset:¬†Vector3 ) ‚Üí¬†() Translates the bullet by an offset in world space. Equivalent toSetPosition(GetPosition() + offset).

searchDocs.title: AddVelocity‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> VetraCast:AddVelocity(delta:¬†Vector3 ) ‚Üí¬†() Adds a delta to the bullet's current velocity. Useful for impulse effects such as explosion knockback applied mid-flight.

searchDocs.title: AddAcceleration‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> VetraCast:AddAcceleration(delta:¬†Vector3 ) ‚Üí¬†() Adds a delta to the bullet's constant acceleration. Useful for variable wind or thrust that builds up over time via repeated calls.

searchDocs.title: ResetBounceState‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> VetraCast:ResetBounceState() ‚Üí¬†() Resets the corner-trap sentinel fields (LastBounceTime, LastBounceNormal, LastBouncePosition) back to their initial values. Call this after any programmatic mid-flight velocity change (e.g. SetVelocity, AddVelocity) that deliberately reverses or sharply redirects the bullet, otherwise the corner-trap detector may falsely terminate the cast on the next bounce.

searchDocs.title: BehaviorBuilder‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> Vetra.BehaviorBuilder: BehaviorBuilder Re-export of the BehaviorBuilder module so consumers only need to require Vetra and can access the builder via Vetra.BehaviorBuilder.

searchDocs.title: new‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> Vetra.new() ‚Üí¬†Vetra Creates a new Vetra instance and connects the per-frame simulation loop to the appropriate RunService event (Heartbeat on server, RenderStepped on client). CAUTION Call this only once. Calling new() more than once will log a warning and return an additional instance sharing the same underlying state ‚ÄĒ the frame loop is not connected a second time.

searchDocs.title: Fire‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> Vetra:Fire( context:¬†BulletContext,-- The public bullet object weapon code interacts with. behavior:¬†VetraBehavior?-- Optional behavior overrides. Omitted fields use defaults. ) ‚Üí¬†VetraCast-- The internal cast object, or nil if validation failed. Creates and registers a new in-flight projectile cast. context must have non-nil, finite Origin (Vector3), Direction (Vector3), and Speed (number) fields. Any field omitted from behavior falls back to the built-in defaults. After Fire returns the cast is live and will be advanced every frame until it hits something, expires by distance or speed, or is stopped via context:Terminate().

searchDocs.title: GetSignals‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> Vetra:GetSignals() ‚Üí¬†{ OnHit:¬†Signal, OnTravel:¬†Signal, OnPierce:¬†Signal, OnBounce:¬†Signal, OnTerminated:¬†Signal } Returns the module-level Signals table. Connect to these once during weapon initialisation. Every signal passes theBulletContext as its first argument so you can identify the bullet and access its UserData. local Signals = Solver:GetSignals() Signals.OnHit:Connect(function(context, result, velocity) if result then -- physical surface impact else -- distance or speed expiry end end) Signals.OnBounce:Connect(function(context, result, velocity, bounceCount) print("Bounce #" .. bounceCount) end) Signal contracts: Signal Arguments OnHit context, result: RaycastResult?, velocity: Vector3 OnTravel context, position: Vector3, velocity: Vector3 OnPierce context, result: RaycastResult, velocity: Vector3, pierceCount: number OnBounce context, result: RaycastResult, velocity: Vector3, bounceCount: number OnTerminated context OnHit fires with a nil result when the bullet expires by distance or minimum speed rather than a physical surface impact. Check result ~= nil to distinguish the two cases. OnTravel fires every frame using Fire (not FireSafe) ‚ÄĒ handlers must not throw.

searchDocs.title: Destroy‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> Vetra:Destroy() ‚Üí¬†() Tears down this solver instance completely. After this call the instance is inert ‚ÄĒ its frame loop is disconnected, all live casts are terminated, all signals are destroyed, and all internal state is cleared. OnTerminated fires for every live cast during shutdown, giving consumers a final cleanup callback consistent with normal cast expiry. DANGER Calling any method on the instance after Destroy() returns is undefined behaviour. The metatable is stripped and the table is frozen ‚ÄĒ all reads and writes will error immediately at the call site. CAUTION Destroy() is not safe to call twice. Guard against this with a flag on your own code if double-destruction is possible at your call sites.

searchDocs.title: Id‚Äč
searchDocs.content: This item is read only and cannot be modified. Read Only </> BulletContext.Id: number Auto-incrementing unique identifier assigned at construction time. Use this to distinguish bullets in signal handlers without storing separate references.

searchDocs.title: Origin‚Äč
searchDocs.content: This item is read only and cannot be modified. Read Only </> BulletContext.Origin: Vector3  World-space muzzle position at the moment the bullet was fired. Never changes.

searchDocs.title: Direction‚Äč
searchDocs.content: This item is read only and cannot be modified. Read Only </> BulletContext.Direction: Vector3  Unit direction vector the bullet was fired along. Never changes.

searchDocs.title: Speed‚Äč
searchDocs.content: This item is read only and cannot be modified. Read Only </> BulletContext.Speed: number Initial speed in studs per second. The bullet's actual speed decreases over time due to bounces (restitution) and pierces (speed retention).

searchDocs.title: StartTime‚Äč
searchDocs.content: This item is read only and cannot be modified. Read Only </> BulletContext.StartTime: number os.clock() timestamp at construction. Use with BulletContext:GetLifetime to compute bullet age without storing the time yourself.

searchDocs.title: Position‚Äč
searchDocs.content: This item is read only and cannot be modified. Read Only </> BulletContext.Position: Vector3?  Current world-space position. Updated every frame by the solver. nil until the first simulation step.

searchDocs.title: Velocity‚Äč
searchDocs.content: This item is read only and cannot be modified. Read Only </> BulletContext.Velocity: Vector3  Current velocity vector in studs per second. Updated every frame by the solver. The magnitude of this vector is the current speed.

searchDocs.title: Alive‚Äč
searchDocs.content: This item is read only and cannot be modified. Read Only </> BulletContext.Alive: boolean true while the cast is being simulated. Set to false by BulletContext:Terminate or automatically by the solver on hit, distance expiry, or speed expiry.

searchDocs.title: Length‚Äč
searchDocs.content: This item is read only and cannot be modified. Read Only </> BulletContext.Length: number Cumulative distance the bullet has travelled along its actual ray path, in studs.

searchDocs.title: UserData‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> BulletContext.UserData: {[any]:¬†any} Free-form table for attaching cast-specific metadata (shooter UserId, weapon type, damage value, etc.). Surfaced on every signal emission ‚ÄĒ read it in your handlers to route events without maintaining a parallel lookup. context.UserData.Damage = 45 context.UserData.ShooterId = Players.LocalPlayer.UserId

searchDocs.title: new‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> BulletContext.new(config:¬†BulletContextConfig) ‚Üí¬†BulletContext Creates a new BulletContext. Origin, Direction, and Speed are required. Callbacks and SolverData are optional and primarily used internally.

searchDocs.title: IsAlive‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> BulletContext:IsAlive() ‚Üí¬†boolean Returns whether the bullet is still alive and being simulated.

searchDocs.title: GetLifetime‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> BulletContext:GetLifetime() ‚Üí¬†number-- Age in seconds. Returns how many seconds have elapsed since the bullet was created.

searchDocs.title: GetDistanceTraveled‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> BulletContext:GetDistanceTraveled() ‚Üí¬†number-- Distance in studs, or 0 if no position is recorded yet. Returns the straight-line distance from BulletContext.Origin to the current BulletContext.Position. Note: this is not the same as BulletContext.Length, which accumulates the actual curved path length.

searchDocs.title: GetSnapshot‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> BulletContext:GetSnapshot() ‚Üí¬†BulletSnapshot Returns a read-only snapshot of the bullet's current state. Useful for logging, replication, or passing state to systems that should not hold a live reference to the context.

searchDocs.title: Terminate‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> BulletContext:Terminate() ‚Üí¬†() Terminates the bullet immediately, notifying the solver to clean up resources, destroy the cosmetic object, and fire OnTerminated. Calling Terminate on an already-dead bullet is a no-op.

searchDocs.title: SegmentSize‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> HighFidelityBuilder:SegmentSize(value:¬†number) ‚Üí¬†HighFidelityBuilder Starting sub-segment length in studs. Smaller values produce more raycasts per frame and better thin-surface detection at the cost of performance. Adjusted adaptively at runtime. Default: 0.5

searchDocs.title: FrameBudget‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> HighFidelityBuilder:FrameBudget(value:¬†number) ‚Üí¬†HighFidelityBuilder Target wall-clock budget in milliseconds this cast may spend on sub-segment raycasts per frame. The adaptive system scales segment size up or down to stay near this target. Default: 4

searchDocs.title: AdaptiveScale‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> HighFidelityBuilder:AdaptiveScale(value:¬†number) ‚Üí¬†HighFidelityBuilder Multiplier applied when coarsening or refining segment size adaptively. Must be > 1. Higher values adapt faster but with less precision. Default: 1.5

searchDocs.title: MinSegmentSize‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> HighFidelityBuilder:MinSegmentSize(value:¬†number) ‚Üí¬†HighFidelityBuilder Hard floor for adaptive segment size reduction in studs. Must be <= SegmentSize. Prevents the adaptive algorithm from shrinking segments to near-zero. Default: 0.1

searchDocs.title: MaxBouncesPerFrame‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> HighFidelityBuilder:MaxBouncesPerFrame(value:¬†number) ‚Üí¬†HighFidelityBuilder Maximum bounces allowed across all sub-segments within a single frame step. Prevents a bullet from exhausting its entire lifetime bounce budget in one frame. Default: 10

searchDocs.title: Done‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> HighFidelityBuilder:Done() ‚Üí¬†BehaviorBuilder Returns the root BehaviorBuilder.
